name: MiMo-V2.5
archDisplay: Sparse MoE (256 routed experts, 8/token), hybrid Sliding-Window+Global attention 5:1, native 729M-param ViT + 261M-param audio encoder, 3x MTP modules
note: Xiaomi's native omnimodal flagship-tier model (text/image/video/audio), 310B total / 15B active MoE, 1M context, fully open-sourced under MIT. Consolidates the prior separate MiMo-V2-Pro (reasoning) and MiMo-V2-Omni (multimodal) lines into one architecture built on the MiMo-V2-Flash backbone.

results.notes: SWE-Bench Pro ('Real-World Bug Fixing'); read directly from the embedded benchmark-chart JS data (codingData array) on Xiaomi's official MiMo-V2.5 product page.
Terminal-Bench 2.0 ('Terminal Task Completion'); from the same embedded chart data.
MMMU-Pro ('Image Understanding & Reasoning'); from the embedded multimodalData chart array.
Video-MME ('General Video QA'); from the embedded multimodalData chart array.
CharXiv 'RQ' (reasoning-question) subset, chart-understanding benchmark; from the embedded multimodalData chart array.

Official PinchBench (OpenClaw agent benchmark) leaderboard average score for MiMo-V2.5, ranked #6 of 50 official models. Confirmed on PinchBench's own site, distinct from the MiMo-V2.5-Pro entry on the same leaderboard.
Independent (Artificial-Analysis-sourced) IFBench score for MiMo-V2.5 via Kilo Code's model page. Vendor (Xiaomi) does not publish this benchmark itself.

GPQA Diamond (graduate-level scientific reasoning), shown on OpenRouter's Benchmarks tab for the xiaomi/mimo-v2.5 model page (matches this model exactly: MIT, $0.105-0.14/$0.28, 1M context, Apr 22 2026 release). Data is sourced from Artificial Analysis's independent evaluation; cross-checked against artificialanalysis.ai/models/mimo-v2-5-0424 which shows the same figure rounded to 85%, and against the MiMo-V2.5-Pro comparison page which shows Pro at a distinct, higher 87% -- confirming no variant conflation with the Pro model.

Humanity's Last Exam, from OpenRouter's Benchmarks tab (Artificial-Analysis-sourced) for xiaomi/mimo-v2.5. Cross-checked against artificialanalysis.ai/models/mimo-v2-5-0424 (rounded to 25%) and against the head-to-head MiMo-V2.5-Pro-vs-MiMo-V2.5 comparison page, which shows Pro at a distinct, higher 34% -- confirming this figure is for the non-Pro model.

GDPval-AA v2 raw Elo (anchored to a human baseline of 1000), read directly off Artificial Analysis's dedicated GDPval-AA v2 leaderboard chart after adding 'MiMo-V2.5, Xiaomi' via the page's own model picker -- distinct from MiMo-V2.5-Pro's 1265 on the same chart. (AA's model-overview pages additionally show this as a normalized '32%' via their internal (Elo-500)/2000 transform, which is NOT the unit this corpus's gdpval-aa benchmark uses -- the corpus's existing gdpval-aa entries for other models are all raw Elo in the ~800-1700 range, so the raw 1145 value was used instead.)

τ²-Bench Telecom (Sierra Research's dual-control conversational-agent benchmark), from OpenRouter's Benchmarks tab, Artificial-Analysis-sourced, part of AA's Agentic Index (GDPval-AA + τ²-Bench Telecom average) for MiMo-V2.5. Not to be confused with AA's newer, harder τ³-Banking eval (MiMo-V2.5 scores a much lower 7% there) -- that is a genuinely different benchmark/domain and was deliberately NOT mapped to this or any taxonomy slug.
LiveCodeBench, independently benchmarked by Vals AI for MiMo V2.5 (±1.07 CI). Not published by Xiaomi itself for this model.

MMLU-Pro, independently benchmarked by Vals AI for MiMo V2.5 (±0.37 CI). Not published by Xiaomi itself for this model (the MMLU/MMLU-Pro table in the HF README is explicitly the pretrained -Base checkpoint, not this released model).

SWE-bench (distinct from the already-tracked SWE-Bench Pro), independently benchmarked by Vals AI for MiMo V2.5 (±2.03 CI). Not to be confused with the widely-reported 'SWE-bench Verified 78.9%' figure, which is for MiMo-V2.5-Pro, a different (1.02T/42B) model -- deliberately excluded here.

Text Arena (Overall) Elo on arena.ai (formerly LMSYS Chatbot Arena), read directly off the live leaderboard for the 'mimo-v2.5' row specifically (MIT license, $0.10/$0.28, 1M context -- matching this model; explicitly distinct on the same leaderboard from mimo-v2.5-pro at 1466, mimo-v2-pro at 1448, and mimo-v2-omni at 1430). Rank #76 of 374 models, ±5 CI, 39,076 votes, per the Jul 10, 2026 snapshot.

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), OpenClaw harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 8.6% / Score 23.6% (rank 47, listed as 'mimo-v2-5' -- the non-Pro variant). Score component used per corpus convention.

Artificial Analysis's newer, harder τ³-Banking evaluation for MiMo-V2.5, distinct from the tracked τ²-Bench Telecom row. [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.] [score corrected during round-12 accuracy re-verify: 7 -> 6.6, per verifier: The cited URL https://artificialanalysis.ai/models/mimo-v2-5 returns HTTP 404 -- it does not exist. The correct AA slug for this model (per the sibling MiMo-V2.5-Pro file's own verification notes, which reference 'artificialanalysis.ai/models/mimo-v2]

verificationNotes:
Params/architecture/context/modalities/license confirmed directly against the raw HuggingFace README (XiaomiMiMo/MiMo-V2.5), which also gives an internal 'component table' cross-checked against the Pro repo's identical table (both agree: 310B/15B, 48-layer, 5:1 SWA:GA, 3x MTP). Release date corroborated by Wikipedia's Xiaomi MiMo lineage entry, MarkTechPost (published 2026-04-22), and Xiaomi's own blog (one secondary source, GSMArena, says 2026-04-28, and the Pro-specific Xiaomi page separately says 2026-04-27; treated as the same announcement week, with 2026-04-22 taken as authoritative per Wikipedia + the MarkTechPost publish date + this task's brief). IMPORTANT CAVEAT: the benchmark table embedded in the HuggingFace READMEs (GSM8K 83.3, MMLU 86.3, etc.) is explicitly labeled 'Base Model Evaluation' and reports the pretrained -Base checkpoint, not the released chat/agentic model — so those numbers were NOT used for 'results' below. Instead, results were pulled from the live JS-driven benchmark charts embedded in Xiaomi's own product page (https://mimo.xiaomi.com/mimo-v2-5/), fetched and parsed directly from raw HTML/JS (not summarized), which report the actual shipped model against Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro, GPT-5.4, Kimi K2.6, DeepSeek-V4-Flash.

2026-07-16 gap-closing pass on the four missing core benchmarks and two comparability flags: (1) MMLU, MATH, AIME, and HumanEval remain unpublished for the released MiMo-V2.5 chat/agentic model. Checked all three required source types: Xiaomi's own product page (mimo.xiaomi.com/mimo-v2-5/, raw embedded JS benchmark-chart data -- codingData and multimodalData arrays list only MiMo Coding Bench, Claw-Eval Text/Multimodal, Terminal-Bench 2.0, SWE-Bench Pro, CharXiv RQ, MMMU-Pro, HR-Bench 4k, OmniDocBench, Video-MME, DailyOmni, VideoHolmes -- no legacy knowledge/math/code benchmarks at all); OpenRouter's Artificial-Analysis-sourced Benchmarks tab for xiaomi/mimo-v2.5 (Reasoning/Coding/Knowledge sections: GPQA Diamond, HLE, IFBench, tau2-Bench Telecom, AA-LCR, GDPval-AA, CritPt, SciCode, Terminal-Bench Hard, AA-Omniscience Accuracy/Non-Hallucination -- confirmed via live browser render, no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval) and artificialanalysis.ai/models/mimo-v2-5-0424 directly (same benchmark set); and Vals AI's model page (vals.ai/models/xiaomi_mimo-v2.5: Vals/Multimodal Index, Code Migration, CorpFin v2, Finance Agent v2, MedCode, MedScribe, MortgageTax, ProofBench, SAGE, TaxEval v2, Vibe Code Bench, GPQA Diamond, LiveCodeBench, LegalBench, MMLU-Pro, MMMU[-Pro], SWE-bench[-Verified], Terminal-Bench 2.1 -- no plain MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval). Cross-referenced sibling MiMo-V2.5-Pro's corpus file, whose own verificationNotes independently document the identical pattern: its HuggingFace README's MMLU/GSM8K/AIME-style table is explicitly the pretrained -Base checkpoint and was deliberately excluded from that model's results too, and no independent tracker (AA, OpenRouter, Vals AI) back-fills these for the Pro chat model either. Treated as a genuine, expected absence -- Xiaomi does not publish these largely-saturated legacy benchmarks for its V2.5-generation post-trained models -- rather than guessed/estimated. (2) The mmmu=80 entry (sourced from Vals AI) was confirmed mislabeled: vals.ai/benchmarks/mmmu's own page <title> reads "MMMU Pro" (verified via raw HTML fetch, not just the rendered summary), meaning this score is actually Vals AI's MMMU-Pro result, duplicating the model's already-present, correctly-slugged mmmu-pro entry (77.9, self-reported from Xiaomi's own product page). Per the corpus's duplicate-resolution rule, the mislabeled mmmu=80 row was dropped rather than creating a second mmmu-pro entry for the same underlying evaluation. (3) The swe-bench=71 entry (also sourced from Vals AI) was re-examined and found to already be correctly slugged, contrary to the flag: vals.ai/benchmarks/swebench's own page <title> reads "SWE-bench Verified" (confirmed via raw HTML fetch, repeated 3x in visible page text, zero occurrences of "SWE-bench Pro" anywhere on the page), and this corpus's own benchmarks.json defines the swe-bench slug's canonical name as "SWE-bench Verified" ("Resolving real GitHub issues in real repositories, verified subset"). The existing notes already correctly distinguished this score from both SWE-Bench Pro and from MiMo-V2.5-Pro's SWE-bench Verified 78.9% -- no relabel or duplicate-drop was warranted; the notes field was only lightly extended to record this 2026-07-16 re-verification for future auditors.

2026-07-18 gap-chase for 'mmmu' (plain MMMU, distinct from the already-tracked mmmu-pro=77.9): First confirmed via capabilities.vision=true and modalities including 'vision'/'video'/'audio' that this model is NOT text-only (unlike sibling MiMo-V2.5-Pro, which is correctly marked architecturally-inapplicable for mmmu in its own file) -- so MMMU is applicable in principle and any absence must be a genuine reporting gap, not an architecture mismatch. Searched all required method categories:

(1) Official launch materials, re-checked at the pixel level beyond the prior JS/HTML-text parse: downloaded and directly viewed the actual PNG chart embedded in the HuggingFace README (huggingface.co/XiaomiMiMo/MiMo-V2.5/resolve/main/assets/mimo-v2.5-multimodal-bench.png), which backs the README's '### Multimodal Benchmarks' section and was previously invisible to text-only fetches since it's a raster image, not DOM text. The chart shows exactly 8 multimodal benchmarks for MiMo-V2.5 vs. MiMo-V2-Omni/Kimi K2.6/Claude Opus 4.6/Claude Sonnet 4.6/Gemini 3 Pro/GPT-5.4: CharXiv RQ 81.0, MMMU-Pro 77.9, HR-Bench(4k) 88.5, OmniDocBench 87.2, Claw-Eval Multimodal 23.8, Video-MME 87.7, DailyOmni 83.5, VideoHolmes 64.0 -- MMMU-Pro is the only MMMU-family bar present; there is no plain-MMMU bar anywhere on the official chart. Re-confirmed via raw-HTML grep of Xiaomi's own product page (mimo.xiaomi.com/mimo-v2-5/) that only the literal string 'MMMU-Pro' occurs, never bare 'MMMU'.

(2) Independent trackers, re-checked at the raw-payload level: Artificial Analysis's full embedded JSON payload for this model (artificialanalysis.ai/models/mimo-v2-5-0424, ~3.2MB fetch) and its DeepSeek-V4-Flash head-to-head comparison page contain only 'mmmu-pro'/'mmmuPro' fields -- zero occurrences of a distinct plain-'mmmu' field in either. Vals AI's model page (vals.ai/models/xiaomi_mimo-v2.5) does show a row visually labeled 'MMMU' (target-width 80%, rank 34/82), but its href points to /benchmarks/mmmu, and that benchmark page's own <title> tag reads 'MMMU Pro' with benchmarkName 'MMMU Pro' in its embedded data -- Vals AI's 'mmmu' slug is internally an alias for MMMU-Pro, not a separate eval. This is the same 80% figure already investigated and deliberately dropped from this file in an earlier round as a mislabeled duplicate of the tracked mmmu-pro=77.9 -- re-confirmed here, not re-added. llm-stats.com/models/mimo-v2.5 lists 'mmmu' only as a generic footer/glossary link to its benchmark-definitions page with zero associated score for this model in its embedded JSON (only 'mmmu-pro' carries an actual benchmark_id + score for this model). BenchLM.ai's model page was fetched and explicitly has no separate MMMU (non-Pro) row, only MMMU-Pro=77.9. OpenRouter's Benchmarks tab mirrors Artificial-Analysis-sourced data per this file's existing methodology; its client-rendered JSON wasn't directly fetchable via static curl, but its upstream AA source was exhaustively checked and has no plain-mmmu field, so OpenRouter is very unlikely to surface one either.

(3) Academic/arXiv: no dedicated MiMo-V2.5 technical-report paper exists on arXiv as of this search (only MiMo-VL, MiMo-V2-Flash, and MiMo-Embodied reports were found, all predating or unrelated to this exact model/release). A GitHub/web search surfaced an MMMU figure of 70.6 for 'MiMo-VL-7B-RL-2508' -- a much older, smaller, unrelated model in a different MiMo sub-line -- not applicable to MiMo-V2.5 and not used.

(4) GitHub eval-harness logs: Xiaomi's own eval-framework repo (XiaomiMiMo/lmms-eval) exists, but no committed results.json or run log specific to MiMo-V2.5 with an MMMU score was found.

(5) Sibling-model comparison tables: searched for MiMo-V2.5 MMMU figures appearing in GLM-5.1/Kimi-K2.6/DeepSeek-V4 launch-comparison articles; none surfaced a plain-MMMU number for this model (comparisons found were limited to BenchAlign/coding aggregates).

(6) HF community discussions: XiaomiMiMo/MiMo-V2.5's Hugging Face discussions page was checked directly; no MMMU-related community submission was found.

Conclusion: every source that reports an MMMU-family benchmark for this model (Xiaomi's own official chart, Artificial Analysis, Vals AI, llm-stats.com) reports MMMU-Pro specifically, and none report the plain/legacy MMMU eval -- consistent with MMMU-Pro having functionally superseded plain MMMU in this model generation's public reporting (mirroring the already-documented MMLU-Pro-over-MMLU pattern this file records for text benchmarks). Treated as a genuine, thoroughly-verified absence rather than an unsearched gap -- omitted_no_evidence rather than estimated/invented. No existing result rows were altered by this pass.
